name: harness-forge
description: Hydrate a project to run looping, latticed agentic workflows. A kernel that scaffolds and operates a typed knowledge lattice â€” nine declarative layers Ă— five scopes Ă— a maturity state machine â€” driven by the engine (defineâ†’createâ†’validate at the smallest signal-yielding scope, the signal minted from a real verifier command's exit status), the compass (scanâ†’rank), and the regeneration loop (operateâ†’ledgerâ†’distillâ†’patterns). Runs a BOUNDED autonomous loop (/harness-run): a harness-builder orchestrator advances the frontier, with the caps (max-cells/iterations/wall-clock) and the no-progress stop ENFORCED IN CODE by a wired budget stop-gate â€” once a run is armed (the orchestrator's step 0), the loop cannot write past its budget. Ships an operating agent roster (a harness-builder orchestrator Â· harness-advancer Â· harness-auditor Â· harness-distiller) plus a 7-critic structural council (partial-order Â· verifier-integrity Â· reward-hacking Â· naming Â· budget Â· autonomy Â· staleness, fanned out in parallel isolated contexts by a harness-council orchestrator), stdlib gates that mechanize typed naming, the dependency partial-order, lattice structural validation, staleness propagation, the no-progress detector, and the global run budget, a consent-gated installer (wire.py) that wires the blocking protected-verifier gate, the budget stop-gate, and the audit-trail + staleness-cascade hooks into the project's own worker loop, an advisory session hook that flags naming/staleness on save (never blocks), a /harness-status operator dashboard, and a read-only lattice-query MCP over the project's .harness/ state. Self-contained.

harness_dir.title: Harness state directory
harness_dir.description: Path to the project's `.harness/` directory (the durable lattice state â€” lattice.json, the layer dirs, signals/, ledger/) that the read-only lattice-query MCP reads. Optional â€” leave unset to run the engine, compass, gates, and agent roster without live retrieval.
